claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents with dry-run default, explicit authorization requirements, and git-backed file snapshot verification.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot (lines 97-109) and diffBoundarySnapshots (lines 111-121) implement file-level boundary audit using git ls-files and filesystem walks
evidence.why: Core boundary enforcement mechanism tracks project file mutations before/after agent execution

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot runs before agent execution at line 516 to capture baseline state
evidence.why: Pre-execution snapshot establishes baseline for comparison

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit runs at lines 566-572, comparing before/after snapshots to detect violations
evidence.why: Post-execution boundary check ensures agents did not mutate files outside allowed roots

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers added at lines 585-597 when boundaryChanges are detected, blocking PRD readiness
evidence.why: Boundary violations block PRD readiness even when agent results pass
Default safety prevents accidental agent execution without user authorization

evidence.summary: Execute mode checks require both executeAgents (lines 475-480) and allowAgentDispatch (lines 481-484) flags to be true, with early return if either is missing (lines 509-510)
evidence.why: Dual-flag requirement ensures intentional authorization for agent dispatch

evidence.summary: gitFiles function uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' at lines 62-69 for git-backed boundary snapshots
evidence.why: Git integration detects untracked and modified project files during boundary audit

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors at lines 151-158 validates that evidence records declare scope as project/external/user/unknown
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation prevents assumptions from being promoted to project facts

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION at lines 37-50 defines required evidence structure with scope validation
evidence.why: Schema contract enforces evidence discipline and scope separation

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues at lines 513-530 checks that project facts include concrete file locators
evidence.why: Project evidence without paths blocks readiness to prevent vague claims

evidence.summary: Test at lines 381-414 verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test coverage confirms boundary enforcement overrides successful agent results

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary tests at lines 416-452 detect untracked and gitignored project mutations
evidence.why: Git integration tests verify boundary audit catches all file changes

evidence.summary: Evidence scope validation tests at lines 340-385 ensure external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts and project evidence requires concrete locator
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation tests ensure project facts require project-scoped evidence

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation at lines 77-98 creates claude -p invocations with permission-mode, settings, and tool restriction flags
evidence.why: Provider adapter configures tool boundaries for evidence agents

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt at lines 258-328 generates agent instructions with boundary rules and evidence schema
evidence.why: Agent prompt enforces write boundary and evidence structure requirements

risks: If boundary audit has filesystem race conditions or git state timing issues, violations could escape detection
If provider adapter tool configuration fails to enforce boundaries, agents could mutate project files despite audit

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer evidence claims. Implementation is complete with git-backed snapshots, dry-run defaults, dual-flag authorization, scope validation, and comprehensive test coverage. No contradictions found.
